description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

entity: European Theatre of World War II
surface_forms: European Theatre of World War II
description: huge area of heavy fighting across Europe
description_wiki: The European theatre of World War II was one of the two main theatres of combat during World War II, taking place from September 1939 to May 1945.

description: city in Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: McKeesport is a city in Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, United States.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: largest city in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Philadelphia (  FIL-ə-DEL-fee-ə), colloquially referred to as Philly, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Pennsylvania, and the sixth-most populous city in the United States, with a Census-estimated population of 1,574,281 in July 2025.

description: U.S. military facility in New Jersey
description_wiki: Camp Kilmer is a former United States Army camp in Central New Jersey that was activated in June 1942 as a staging area and part of an installation of the New York Port of Embarkation.

description: United States Army installation in Kentucky
description_wiki: Fort Campbell is a United States Army installation located between the Kentucky–Tennessee border between Hopkinsville, Kentucky and Clarksville, Tennessee.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Kentucky (US:  , UK: ), officially the Commonwealth of Kentucky, is a landlocked state in the Southeastern region of the United States.

description: United States Armed Forces Training Station
description_wiki: Fort Sill is a major United States Army post north of Lawton, Oklahoma, approximately 85 miles (137 km) southwest of Oklahoma City, established in 1869 during the Indian Wars.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Oklahoma (  OH-klə-HOH-mə; Choctaw: Oklahumma, pronounced [oklahómma]) is a landlocked state in the South Central and Southwestern region of the United States.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: mountain range in Oklahoma, United States
description_wiki: The Wichita Mountains are located in the southwestern portion of the U.S. state of Oklahoma.

description: country in southern Europe
description_wiki: Italy, officially the Italian Republic, is a country in Southern and Western Europe.

description: country in the Horn of Africa
description_wiki: Ethiopia, officially the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia, is a landlocked country located in the Horn of Africa region of East Africa.

description: war between the Republicans and the Nationalists in Spain from 1936 to 1939
description_wiki: The Spanish Civil War (Spanish: La guerra civil española) was fought from 1936 to 1939 between the Republicans and the Nationalist rebels.

description: ethnic group
description_wiki: Greek Americans are Americans of full or partial Greek ancestry.

description: mixed brigade that fought for the Spanish Republic in the Spanish Civil War as a part of the International Brigades
country in Southeast Europe
description_wiki: Greece, officially the Hellenic Republic, is a country in Southeast Europe.

description: country in West Asia and Southeast Europe
description_wiki: Turkey, officially the Republic of Türkiye, is a country mainly located in Anatolia in West Asia, with a smaller part called East Thrace in Southeast Europe.

description: 1944-1945 World War II battle
description_wiki: The Battle of the Bulge, also known as the Ardennes Offensive, was the last major German offensive campaign on the Western Front during the Second World War, taking place from 16 December 1944 to 25 January 1945.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
United States Army general, military historian, diplomat (1922–2013)
description_wiki: John Sheldon Doud Eisenhower (August 3, 1922 – December 21, 2013) was a United States Army officer, diplomat, and military historian.

description: line of fortifications along the French/German border
description_wiki: The Maginot Line (; French: Ligne Maginot [liɲ maʒino]), named after the French Minister of War André Maginot, is a line of concrete fortifications, obstacles and weapon installations built by France in the 1930s.

description: president of the United States from 1953 to 1961 (1890–1969)
description_wiki: Dwight David "Ike" Eisenhower (born David Dwight Eisenhower; October 14, 1890 – March 28, 1969) was the 34th president of the United States, serving from 1953 to 1961.

description: 1871–1918 territory of the German Empire
German defensive line used in World War II
description_wiki: The Siegfried Line, known in German as the Westwall ([ˈvɛstˌval], 'western rampart'), was a German defensive line built during the late 1930s.

description: river in Western Europe to the North Sea
description_wiki: The Rhine ( RYNE) is one of the major rivers of Europe.

description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

description: formation of the United States Army (1918/19, 1932-74 and since 1982)
United States Army general (1885–1945)
description_wiki: George Smith Patton Jr.
